content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

November 21, 2003


The Honorable Mark Wyland
California Assembly
Post Office Box 942849
Sacramento, CA 94249-0074

Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-03-248

Dear Assemblymember Wyland:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ As I discussed with your office on November 7, 2003, the Act requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to a person whose duties under the Act are in question, or to that person's authorized representative. However, the Commission does not provide advice to a third party about a public official's duties under the Act. (Section 83114; regulation 18329, copy enclosed.) Thus, we cannot respond to your specific questions and do not expressly discuss them herein. However, we can provide informal assistance with respect to prospective conduct. Informal assistance does not provide the requestor herewith the immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice. (Section 83114; regulation 18329(c)(3).)

QUESTION

Should you win election to office, may you continue to raise money into that committee after the election?

CONCLUSION

Once the election is over, you may only raise funds to pay your net debt. No other fundraising is permitted.


¹ Government Code sections 81000 – 91014. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.

File No. 1-03-248
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are currently serving your second term in the Assembly and will be seeking a
third term in 2004.

DISCUSSION

Proposition 34, passed by the voters at the statewide general election of
November 2000, added contribution limits, voluntary expenditure limits and made other
major changes to campaigning for office under the Political Reform Act.

Campaigning for Reelection

Candidates for state office may open an account and committee for reelection to a
third term in the Assembly. However, candidates for elected state office, including the
Assembly, may not redesignate campaign committees to run for election for the same
office even if it is for a different term. Once a campaign committee and account are
established, all contributions raised into the future election committee are subject to the
contribution limits for that election.

Post-election Fundraising

If you are elected to your third term, section 85316 prohibits you from raising
funds after the election for purposes other than paying net debt. (See regulation 18531.6,
copy enclosed.) The Act does not provide any specific method for officeholders to raise
funds for officeholder expenses. However, officeholder expenses may be paid from any
committee established for the office that you hold. (Regulation 18525(b).)

For your further information, we have enclosed a copy of the Commission's
publication "Proposition 34 Reporting Information for State Candidates FPPC Fact Sheet
34-01, Volume 3."

If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant General Counsel

Enclosures
JWW:jg
